codeReview.summary: The host library/editor implementation covers the requested CRUD, search, identity recovery, and host/identity separation behaviors overall, but there is a blocking state-management bug in the delete-confirmation flow.

issues.description: Canceling the delete-confirmation screen rebuilds the editor from the persisted host via `HostEditorDraft.from(currentDestination.host, identities)` instead of restoring the in-progress draft. Any unsaved edits made before tapping Delete (for example changed label/address/username/auth mode) are discarded, which violates the expected cancellation behavior that edits apply only after save and cancellations preserve prior editor state.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `d52bf75c134688be766569fbaac885c44c8af7f7`. The implementation is broadly aligned with the feature goals, but canceling delete confirmation drops unsaved host-editor changes, which is a blocking correctness issue.
